steps.title: Identify the subject number
steps.content: The subject is “The Proto-Uralic language”—a singular noun. The intervening phrase “common ancestor of thirty-seven Eurasian languages with similar linguistic properties” is an appositive modifier and doesn't change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires the verb to match the subject's number. Only D) “is” is singular; A) “are,” B) “were,” and C) “have been” are all plural forms that violate this rule. That matches choice D — D is correct.
